Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jon B. Eisenberg, Esq. of the Law Office of Jon B. Eisenberg, T:707.395.0111, for plaintiff/moving party;
Heather Hoesterey, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, T:415.510.3462, and Lucy Wang, Esq, Deputy Attorney General, T:415.510.3543, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. Plaintiff's counsel also informs the Court that since the defendant will not stipulate, he intends to file a Motion to Amend. Therefore, the Court rules on PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS as follows:

The motion is off calendar. Actual discovery and discovery motions are stayed until such time the Court rules on Plaintiff's Motion To Amend.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare a proposed order that repeats the substantive portion of the court's ruling as stated on the record, and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
